steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence says Le Guin wrote both science fiction and fantasy. The second says fans of both genres became her readers. A transition word is needed that shows the second sentence follows logically from the first.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Alternatively': signals a different option to consider. There is no alternative being offered here. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Accordingly': signals a logical result. Because she wrote both genres, fans of both naturally followed — this fits exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Nevertheless': signals a contrast or concession. No contrast exists between the two sentences. ✗
  • Choice D — 'At that time': signals a time reference. The second sentence is not making a point about timing. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
